title: Movable emission control system for auxiliary diesel engines
abstract: A mobile emissions control system having an emission capturing system and emission control system is provided for diesel engines operated on ocean-going ships at-berth. The emissions control system may be mounted on a towable chassis or mounted on a barge, allowing it to be placed alongside ocean-going ships at-berth. A crane or boom transfers a duct of the emissions capturing system extending from the emissions control system to the ship to capture exhaust from its engine. Alternatively, the system may be mounted on an automated guided vehicle (AGV) equipped with a tower and a crane. The crane mounted on the AGV then lifts the duct forming part of the emissions capture system to the ship's exhaust system to capture exhaust from the ship's diesel engine and transfers it to the emissions control system, which cleans the exhaust and then passes clean air into the atmosphere through an exhaust outlet.
claims:
Claims IV. Claims What is claimed is: 1. An emissions reduction system for reducing emissions from a shipâs exhaust, the emissions reduction system comprising: (a) an emissions control system positioned within a housing; (b) an emissions capturing system having a duct; and (c) an automated guided vehicle where the emissions control system is mounted on the automated guided vehicle. 2. The emissions reduction system of claim 1, where the duct has two ends, where one end of the duct extends to the ship to capture the shipâs exhaust and the other end of the duct connects to the emissions control system. 3. The emissions reduction system of claim 2, where the duct is a telescopic duct that expands and retracts. 4. The emissions reduction system of claim 3, where the automated guided vehicle includes a tower having a telescopic crane mounted to the tower, where the tower extends the telescopic duct to the ship to capture the shipâs exhaust and filter it through the emissions control system. 5. The emissions reduction system of claim 1, where the emissions control system includes an exhaust filtering system having ceramic filters. 6. The emissions reduction system of claim 1, where the automated guided vehicle is controlled remotely. 7. An automated guided vehicle for carrying an emissions control system, the automated guided vehicle having a chassis, a tower mounted on the chassis and a crane or boom supported by the tower, where the automated guided vehicle and crane can be controlled remotely. 8. The automated guided vehicle of claim 7 where an emissions control system is mounted on the chassis of the automated guided vehicle. 9. The automated guided vehicle of claim 8, where the automated guided vehicle includes a power source and where the power source is shared by the automated guided vehicle and the emissions control system. 10. The automated guided vehicle of claim 7, where the crane is telescopic. 11. The automated guided vehicle of claim 8, where the height of the tower is at least as tall as the height of the emissions control system. 12. The automated guided vehicle of claim 8, where a telescopic duct is attached to the emissions control system and is supported by the crane of the automated guided vehicle. 13. The automated guided vehicle of claim 12, where the crane is capable of extending the telescopic duct to a ship to capture the shipâs exhaust. 14. The automated guided vehicle of claim 8, where the emissions control system includes an exhaust filtering system having ceramic filters. 15. The automated guided vehicle of claim 7, where the crane is pivotally attached to the tower. 16. An emissions reduction system for filtering exhaust from a ship at berth, the emissions reduction system comprising: 

 an automated guided vehicle having a chassis, a tower mounted on the chassis and a crane or boom supported by the tower; an emissions control system mounted on the chassis of the automated guided vehicle; an emissions capturing system having a telescopic duct attached at a first end to the emissions control system and supported by the crane or boom of the automated guided vehicle such that the crane or boom is capable of positioning a second end of the duct near the ship to capture the shipâs exhaust and filter it through the emissions control system. 17. The emissions control system of claim 16, where the automated guided vehicle includes a power source for controlling the movement of the automated guided vehicle and where the emissions control system is also powered by the power source. 18. The emissions control system of claim 16, where the movement of the automated guided vehicle and the movement of the crane are both controlled remotely. 19. The emissions control system of claim 16, where the crane or boom is telescopic. 20. The emissions control system of claim 16, where the emissions control system includes an exhaust filtering system having ceramic filters.
